component.name: Iskra
component.summary: Sermon planning for preachers who work from structured notes

description.p: Iskra (Искра, "spark") is a sermon-planning tool built around structured notes rather than a full manuscript: jot single-line ideas, expand any of them with longer notes, group them into movements, drag everything into order, tag it, and export a clean outline for Rubric's sermon field.

ul.li: Idea bars with click-to-edit text, expandable notes, and idea/part tags
Movements to group ideas, with drag-and-drop reordering within and between them
Continuous auto-numbering across the whole sermon
Revised Common Lectionary readings, season, and liturgical colour resolved from the planned date
Sermon library with search by title, tags, and date
Undo/redo covering every structural and text edit
Git sync — commit and push to all remotes in one click; sign in with GitHub via device flow
Export to a format Rubric imports directly into its sermon field

screenshot.caption: Planning a sermon's movements and ideas

description.p: Kindled Verse — scripture citations with live autocomplete (type @john3:16 to tag and cite a reference, with a Bibliography section in Preaching View and Print), a printable Preaching View, movement split/merge, multi-select ideas, and a batch of workflow shortcuts and visual polish.

First Light — the first release. Sermon templates, a large-print chrome-free Preaching View, an in-app history browser backed by git, sermon series grouping, quick-pick tag chips, and a batch of drag-and-drop and git sync robustness fixes.
